text: vv. 31, 32: The Greek text here varies considerably from the Latin version. It runs: ‘Be never ashamed to confess thy faults; wouldst thou swim against the stream’s force? Never put thyself in the power of a fool, and never flatter a ruler’s greatness’. Throughout the last fourteen verses of this chapter, the Latin and the Greek have many different twists of meaning, and neither can be interpreted with much certainty.
